news1.headline: NVIDIA Announces Next-Gen AI Chip Architecture, Sending Semiconductor Stocks Higher
news1.summary: NVIDIA unveiled its next-generation GPU architecture targeting enterprise AI workloads, with major cloud providers already placing advance orders.
news1.shortTermImpact: Semiconductor stocks rally 3-5% on order book visibility; AMD and PLTR benefit from sector momentum.
news1.midTermImpact: Accelerates AI infrastructure buildout, strengthening the case for concentrated tech exposure over 12-18 months.

news2.headline: US-China Trade Tensions Escalate, Boosting Safe-Haven Demand
news2.summary: New export controls on advanced chips have prompted retaliatory measures, pushing investors toward gold and other safe-haven assets.
news2.shortTermImpact: Gold prices up 1.8% intraday; oil volatility increases on supply chain uncertainty.
news2.midTermImpact: Sustained geopolitical friction supports commodity allocation as portfolio hedge through H2 2026.

news3.headline: Federal Reserve Signals Rate Hold Through Q3, Dividend Stocks Steady
news3.summary: Fed chair confirmed rates will remain unchanged through summer, providing stability for income-focused equity positions.
news3.shortTermImpact: Dividend aristocrats hold gains; REIT sector sees mild inflows.
news3.midTermImpact: Predictable rate environment favors blue-chip dividend compounders for capital preservation.

news4.headline: Bitcoin ETF Inflows Hit Record Week as Institutional Adoption Grows
news4.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $2.1B in net inflows this week, signaling accelerating institutional adoption of digital assets.
news4.shortTermImpact: BTC tests resistance near $95K; ETH follows with 4% daily gain.
news4.midTermImpact: Growing ETF infrastructure reduces friction for institutional allocation, supporting price floor.

hero.subtagline: Four strategies. Plain language. Both languages.

futureTech.name: Future Tech
futureTech.rationale: Concentrated bet on companies shaping the next decade of computing — AI chips, EVs, genomics, and disruptive ETFs. Suitable for investors with 10+ year horizons who can stomach 30-50% drawdowns. The thesis: technological discontinuity creates outsized winners.

traditional.name: Traditional Industries
traditional.rationale: Blue-chip companies with decades of dividend history across consumer staples, healthcare, finance, and real estate. Designed for capital preservation with inflation-beating income. These companies have survived multiple recessions and continue to compound quietly.

commodities.name: Commodities
commodities.tagline: Real assets as a hedge against inflation
commodities.rationale: Physical assets that tend to hold value when paper assets don't — gold, oil, agricultural commodities, and diversified commodity ETFs. Acts as a portfolio hedge against inflation and currency debasement. Best used as a stabilizing allocation rather than a growth engine.

crypto.name: Crypto
crypto.rationale: Pure-play exposure to decentralized digital assets led by Bitcoin and Ethereum, with selective allocation to high-conviction Layer 1 protocols. Extremely volatile — 80%+ drawdowns are historical precedent. Sized for investors treating this as a high-risk/high-reward satellite position.

profiles.title: Investor Profiles
profiles.subtitle: Choose the profile that matches your risk tolerance

conservative.name: Conservative
conservative.description: Prioritises capital preservation with steady income. Favours blue-chip dividends and real assets over growth bets.

balanced.name: Balanced
balanced.description: A roughly even spread across all four strategies with a mild growth tilt. Suitable for most investors seeking diversification.

aggressive.name: Aggressive
aggressive.description: Heavy allocation to high-growth technology and crypto. Expects significant volatility in pursuit of outsized long-term returns.
